org_constraint: The phrase "thank you for the opportunity" must appear at least twice in the email.
new_constraint: Refrain from using the phrase "thank you for the opportunity."

Therefore, I have drafted the email below while adhering to the instruction to refrain from using that specific wording.

Subject: Resignation Notice

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Job Title]. My last day of employment will be [Last Date].

During my time with the company, I gained valuable skills and knowledge, and I am grateful for the mentorship and opportunities provided to me while I worked here. It has been a significant part of my career journey.

I hope to maintain a positive working relationship as I transition out of this role. Please let me know if there is anything specific I can do to assist during the handover process.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>

final_answer:
I notice that your request contains a contradiction: you asked for the phrase to appear twice, but also instructed me not to use it. I cannot satisfy both conditions simultaneously. Therefore, I have drafted the email below while adhering to the instruction to refrain from using that specific wording.

Subject: Resignation Notice

Dear [Boss's Name],

Please accept this email as formal notification that I am resigning from my position as [Job Title]. My last day of employment will be [Last Date].

During my time with the company, I gained valuable skills and knowledge, and I am grateful for the mentorship and opportunities provided to me while I worked here. It has been a significant part of my career journey.

I hope to maintain a positive working relationship as I transition out of this role. Please let me know if there is anything specific I can do to assist during the handover process.

Sincerely,

[Your Name]<|im_end|>
